title: Building This Site with Claude Code (Yes, Including This Post)
excerpt: A meta look at how I used Claude Code to build and style this site — from the initial scaffold to this very post, prompts included.
body:
I've been using [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code) — Anthropic's AI coding agent that runs in your terminal — and this entire site is the result. Not just the styling. The HTML structure, the CSS architecture, the JavaScript blog engine, and yes, this very post.

Here's the honest account of how that went, including the exact prompts I typed.

## What I Was Starting With

The goal was simple: a personal site and blog that I actually own, with no framework magic, no build tools, and no monthly SaaS bill. Just plain HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, deployed to GitHub Pages.

I wanted to write posts in Markdown, keep things fast, and not spend my weekends fighting webpack configs. I build .NET + cloud systems for a living — I know when to reach for complexity and when not to.

## The Initial Build

Claude Code scaffolded the entire thing from a prompt describing what I wanted. It generated:

- `index.html` — homepage with profile photo, hero, and recent posts
- `blog.html` — post listing page
- `post.html` — individual post renderer using [marked.js](https://marked.js.org/) and [highlight.js](https://highlightjs.org/)
- `css/variables.css` — all design tokens in one place
- `css/main.css` — layout, header, footer, typography
- `css/blog.css` — homepage and blog-specific components
- `js/components.js` — shared header/footer rendering
- `js/post.js` — fetches and renders individual posts from JSON files
- `posts/posts.json` — the post manifest

Each post is a JSON file with a `body` field containing Markdown. Clean, no database, no backend. Simple.

## Styling: From Dark to Fun

The first version had a dark theme. Very dark. Like "my IDE at 2am" dark. It was clean but it didn't really feel like *me*.

My prompt to fix it:

> *"I'd like to add some extra styling components to the site. I want it to be fun and reflect my personality more. Less dark themed."*

That's it. One sentence. Claude Code read all the existing CSS files, understood the structure, and came back with:

- A warm cream/sandy color palette — `#fdfaf5` background vs the original `#0a0a0f`. I like to think of it as Arizona desert tones, which feels right for a Sonoran Hot Dog enthusiast.
- **Nunito** as a display font for headings — it's rounded and friendly instead of sterile
- Circular profile photo with a green ring border and a fun tilt-on-hover
- A blinking cursor (`_`) after the "Hi, I'm" greeting — techy and a little dorky, I love it
- **Personality badges** below my tagline: 🏌️ mediocre golfer, 🌭 sonoran hot dog enthusiast, 💻 .net + cloud, 👨‍👩‍👧 family first, 🤖 AI curious
- Pill-shaped nav links and social buttons with a lift effect on hover
- Switched syntax highlighting to the light GitHub theme to match

All from one prompt. The changes were thoughtful — it didn't just slap a light background on things, it read the personality in the existing content and ran with it.

## Writing This Post

My next prompt was:

> *"I want to add a blog post on the site about how I've built it using Claude Code including the prompts."*

And here we are. Claude Code read `post.js` to understand how posts render, checked `posts.json` for the manifest format, wrote this file, and added the entry to the manifest. The whole thing.

## What I Actually Think

A few honest takes after using this for a real project:

**It's faster than you think, in a different way than you think.** I didn't save time by typing less. I saved time by skipping the 45 minutes of "let me look up the right CSS property for this" and "wait, how does `clamp()` work again." The tedious parts just... happened.

**The prompts are embarrassingly short.** "Less dark themed" is not a detailed spec. But because Claude Code reads your actual code before changing anything, it has enough context to make good decisions. It's not guessing from a blank slate — it's working with what you've already built.

**You still have to care.** I reviewed every change. I pushed back on things I didn't like. I'm the one who decided the color palette should have Sonoran Hot Dog vibes. The AI does the typing; you do the thinking.

**Plain tech + AI is a great combo.** Because this site is just HTML/CSS/JS files with no build step, Claude Code can read and modify everything directly. No transpilation, no framework conventions to navigate. The simpler your stack, the more leverage you get from an AI that needs to read and understand it.

## Would I Recommend It?

If you're a developer who wants a personal site without making it a project, yes. The friction is genuinely low.

Now if you'll excuse me, I have a tee time and a hot dog to get to.
